threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 48 bus is reliable. Good luck with that on a Saturday night, mate.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and a bloke started singing opera. Didn't miss a beat, even the driver was tapping along. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a pack of wild boars casually rummaging through the bins at Sonnenallee. That’s when it hit me—this place has its own wild charm.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you have Späti lounges with craft beer on tap, where you can lounge on mismatched sofas while the next-door club thumps away.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they casually side-step the dog on the tracks at Civic Center like it’s just another Tuesday commute.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Only in SF can a Muni bus stop, mid-route, to let a parade of pigeons cross the street. The driver just shook his head and smiled.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí. Really, it’s the local bars and festivals that define the soul of this place.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still pause at the traffic lights, waiting for the green, while everyone else darts across.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look bewildered ordering a flat white at a café, like it’s a complex science experiment rather than just coffee.
Caught the 59 last Saturday; a bloke in full suit argued with the driver about which stop was his ‘unfairly abandoned’ home.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a cat chilling on top of a food truck in Hermannplatz while the owner was off chatting with a friend.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you see people lining up at 3 AM for a kebab like it’s a cultural rite. It’s part of the nightlife ritual here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local when they instinctively know to avoid Civic Center BART when a dog’s on the tracks—always a good excuse to skip that commute.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy feeding pigeons on the tracks at Civic Center yesterday. Muni crew just shrugged and let the chaos unfold. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just one big festival. In reality, the locals are often bemoaning the noise and wishing for a quiet evening.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their confusion over the 'siesta' timing—still trying to get things done during lunch but missing out on the real rhythm of the day.
